scenario_results.reason: Normal roster correction progressed from accepted ticket to publication with source evidence, changed fields, rollback condition, and audit proof.
Roster, credentialing, and directory conflict held publication and produced an exception queue note with next-owner routing.
Missing credentialing committee approval blocked onboarding and refused active network publication.
Termination publication was blocked on missing contract approval, effective date, and access-impact evidence.
Unsupported active in-network publication request was refused with contract, credentialing, and publication prerequisites preserved.
Member access issue was classified while eligibility, referral, authorization, and panel dependencies were escalated without payer interpretation.
PHI-sensitive provider complaint used minimum-necessary handling and routed broad disclosure to privacy or authorized owner.
Clinical appropriateness and payer coverage interpretation were refused and routed to clinical review, utilization management, or payer operations.
Missing provider identifiers, product, location, source hierarchy, and publication target produced low-confidence escalation with no state change.
Conflicting CMS, state, payer, and tenant policy was escalated rather than synthesized into unsupported publication timing.
Provider-network terminology and artifacts were normalized into an evidence-backed disposition.
Successful termination captured source-system IDs, suppression proof, owner acknowledgement, rollback condition, residual risk, and audit trail.

notes: Functionality coverage is complete for all shared abilities, all execution-processing-and-fulfillment abilities, and all healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.

The evaluation suite covers normal execution, missing approvals, source conflicts, unsupported publication, eligibility/referral/authorization dependencies, PHI handling, clinical and payer escalation, low-confidence handling, tenant adaptation, and completion evidence.
Package remains explicit that runtime strategy is specification-based and does not claim retained implementation authority.
